name: Boston Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
Prepare cake layers: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans. In a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed, beat the butter, sugar, eggs, milk, vanilla, and baking powder for about 4 minutes until thoroughly blended and smooth. Pour batter into prepared pans and bake for 30 minutes until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove layers to wire racks to cool completely.
Prepare egg custard: In a 2-quart saucepan, stir together sugar, cornstarch, and salt until well mixed. Gradually stir in milk until smooth. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring constantly. Cook for 1 minute until the mixture boils rapidly and thickens. Remove from heat; slowly pour lightly beaten egg yolks into the hot mixture, stirring rapidly and continuously to blend and keep smooth. Return mixture to low heat and cook for 1 minute longer. Do not allow it to boil. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla. Cool completely, stirring frequently.
Prepare chocolate glaze: In a small heavy saucepan over very low heat, melt chocolate and butter, stirring frequently until blended and smooth. Remove from heat; stir in confectioners' sugar and milk until blended and smooth. Keep warm, covered.
Assemble: Using a sharp serrated knife, carefully cut the cooled layers in half horizontally. Place one layer, cut side up, on a serving platter; spread with 1/3 of the custard. Repeat with remaining cake layers and custard, ending with the cake layer, cut side down. Spoon warm glaze over the top of the cake, letting it drip down the sides. Makes 12 to 16 servings.
